name: Route of the Lakes
name_original: Balaguer - Térmens - Alcoletge - Torrelameu — ruta dels llacs
description: This difficult 31.9-mile touring cycling route through Catalonia's Ruta dels Llacs offers lake views, river scenery, and historic bridges.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Balaguer traverse a diverse landscape, situated between plains and the Montsec Mountain Range, and bisected by the Segre River. The region features hilly terrain, riverbanks, and numerous reservoirs, offering varied cycling experiences. This blend of natural features provides both challenging climbs and flatter, scenic paths. The area is characterized by its mix of mountains, agricultural plains, and calm waters. Best touring cycling routes around Balaguer The most popular touring cycling route is Route of the Lakes, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of several reservoirs, including Camarasa and Sant Llorenç de Montgai. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Segre River – Sant Llorenç de Montgai Lake loop from Balaguer, a difficult 52.6 miles (84.7 km) path. This route follows the Segre River and provides extensive views of Sant Llorenç de Montgai Lake. Local touring cyclists also love the La Mitjana Iron Bridge – Parc de la Mitjana loop from Balaguer, a 38.2 miles (61.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Balaguer is defined by hilly terrain, the Segre River, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Balaguer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Balaguer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Balaguer?
A: Balaguer offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 recorded options. These routes vary in difficulty, length, and elevation gain, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Balaguer?
A: The most popular months for touring cycling in Balaguer are April and September. During these periods, the weather is generally pleasant, making for ideal conditions to explore the diverse landscapes of the Noguera region.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Balaguer?
A: Yes, Balaguer has several easier routes. There are 5 routes classified as easy, and 21 as moderate, providing options for those seeking less challenging rides. These often follow riverbanks or flatter plains, offering scenic views without significant climbs.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on touring cycling routes around Balaguer?
A: Touring cycling routes around Balaguer traverse a diverse landscape. You'll encounter hilly terrain, the banks of the Segre River, and numerous reservoirs like those of Camarasa and Sant Llorenç de Montgai. The region blends mountains, agricultural plains, and calm waters, offering varied scenery from dramatic backdrops to picturesque riverside paths.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Balaguer?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Balaguer are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5330136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Llorenç de Montgai Lake – Segre River loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.2 km route that offers beautiful lake and river views.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history and culture. Routes frequently pass through areas with significant landmarks. You could encounter sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3062857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/947499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montclar Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The city of Balaguer itself boasts a historical center with sites like Plaça del Mercadal Square and the Gothic church of Santa María.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Balaguer offers 27 routes classified as difficult, providing significant challenges for experienced touring cyclists. These routes often feature substantial elevation gains and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10467084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de les Avellanes – Balaguer Bridge loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Balaguer?
A: The touring cycling routes in Balaguer are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views of the Segre River and various reservoirs, and the blend of natural beauty with historical points of interest.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the region's famous reservoirs?
A: Yes, many routes are specifically designed to showcase the region's calm waters and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964960844/route-of-the-lakes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Lakes&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive views of several reservoirs, including Camarasa and Sant Llorenç de Montgai. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segre River – Sant Llorenç de Montgai Lake loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Segre River?
A: Yes, the Segre River is a prominent feature in many touring cycling routes around Balaguer. Routes often follow its banks, providing picturesque and generally flatter options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mitjana Iron Bridge – Parc de la Mitjana loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that leads through riverine landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural features can be explored on these routes, beyond rivers and lakes?
A: Beyond the rivers and lakes, the region around Balaguer offers other natural wonders. You can find dramatic backdrops from the Montsec Mountain Range and explore areas with unique geological formations. The region is also home to wildlife nature reserves, adding to the natural allure.
tips: Emblematic monastery from the 12th century, built at a time of repopulation of territories recovered by the Christians. Balaguer was conquered in 1105, but it was not until the middle of the 12th century that repopulation policies began.

Bridge located in the town of Térmens, in the province of Lérida. For many years it was used to transport beets to the Menàrguens sugar factory (hence the name). When the sugar factory closed, the tracks were removed and it was conditioned to pass all kinds of vehicles.

If our route passes through the town of Alcoletge, we cannot pass by the Church of San Miguel Arcángel de Alcoletge, an important construction from between 1765 and 1773 in neoclassical style with baroque elements.

We are in the town square of Torrelameu, next to the beautiful Romanesque church that was reformed twice more than six centuries ago. Apart from the church, we can take a tour of the old houses of Torrelameu or the Duc mill.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Balaguer - Térmens - Alcoletge - Torrelameu route?
faqs.answer: This route, part of the larger Ruta dels Llacs, features diverse terrain. You'll encounter a mix of existing rural roads, greenways, urban streets, and low-traffic roads. While the elevation gain is moderate, the 'difficult' rating suggests some sections may be unpaved, steep, or technical, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque scenery of 'the wildest west of Catalonia,' running parallel to the 'Tren dels Llacs' panoramic railway. You'll see views of four artificial lakes and follow the course of the Segre river. Notable landmarks include the Cistercian Monastery of Santa María de les Franqueses, the Azucarera Bridge, the Church of San Miguel de Alcoletge, and the Church of Torrelameu.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 51.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours and 23 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' for touring bicycles. It requires good fitness and may include challenging sections, such as unpaved paths or steep climbs, where you might need to push your bike. Beginners might find it too demanding.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers rich riverside forest scenery, which is particularly vibrant in autumn. Spring and early summer would also be excellent, offering pleasant temperatures before the peak heat of summer. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the route connects several towns (Balaguer, Térmens, Alcoletge, Torrelameu), you can start at various points. Balaguer is a common starting point. Look for public parking areas within these towns. The route is also integrated with the FGC train line, offering flexible public transport options to access different sections.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route aren't detailed, generally in Catalonia, dogs are often permitted on trails, especially if they are on a leash. However, given that parts of the route might include urban streets and potentially busy sections, it's advisable to keep your dog leashed and ensure they are comfortable with cycling environments. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Balaguer - Térmens - Alcoletge - Torrelameu section of the Ruta dels Llacs. It utilizes existing public roads and greenways.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily part of the larger Ruta dels Llacs (74%). It also briefly intersects with sections of the TransCatalunya. De Ponent (5%), Etapa 01 (4%), and the Camí del Braçal de Lleida (6%).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Balaguer, Térmens, Alcoletge, and Torrelameu, you will find various amenities. These towns offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it convenient for breaks or overnight stays. Balaguer, being a larger town, will have the most extensive choices.

faqs.question: What lakes will I see along this route?
faqs.answer: The route is part of the 'Route of the Lakes' and offers views of four artificial lakes. While specific names for this segment are not provided, the broader Ruta dels Llacs runs parallel to the 'Tren dels Llacs' and features reservoirs like Cellers. You'll experience landscapes shaped by these water bodies and the Segre river.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and potential for unpaved sections, pack essentials like a repair kit, extra tubes, plenty of water, and snacks. Wear appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet. Sun protection is also recommended. A map or GPS device is useful, as some sections might be less clearly marked.
